paragraphs.text: Freelance
Movement
The Future of Work through Digitalisation

We fully support the objectives of the Digital Fairness Act (DFA) in terms of filling the gaps in existing EU consumer protection law around digital practices, particularly when it comes to minors and vulnerable consumers. We are also aligned with the policy goal of simplifying rules, making enforcement more consistent, reducing legal uncertainty and fragmentation across Member States, as well as ensuring that business compliance costs are proportionate. In short, we want to see the clarification and harmonisation of existing legislation rather than introducing swathes of new rules.

section_path: Digital Fairness Act: Freelance Movement response to the open consultation

paragraphs.text: We would like to see clarity and the predictability of rules - to this end we should have clear definitions (e.g. what constitutes a "dark pattern" or "addictive design", what is "unfair personalisation", "addictive design"). The gaps the DFA fills should be clearly defined and backed by evidence so as to avoid over-prescriptive rules that could have adverse impact on legitimate practices widely used online. We should also ensure harmonisation across EU Member States so platforms operating cross-border can have consistent compliance obligations.

section_path: Digital Fairness Act: Freelance Movement response to the open consultation

evidence_quotes.quote: Designing interfaces to guide choice is not the same as distorting it: they should not be conflated with dark patterns.

paragraphs.text: Design for usability needs to be distinguished from manipulative design - many interface design choices are necessary for usability, clarity, safety, or regulatory compliance (e.g. route suggestions, dropdowns, options). Designing interfaces to guide choice is not the same as distorting it: they should not be conflated with dark patterns. Moreover, new obligations that reduce usability, penalise product improvements, and ultimately undermine the consumer experience should be avoided.

section_path: Digital Fairness Act: Freelance Movement response to the open consultation

paragraphs.text: There is a need to consider the overlap with existing EU rules: Platform Work Directive, DSA (Digital Services Act), DMA (Digital Markets Act), GDPR, AI Act, Unfair Commercial Practices Directive (UCPD) etc. It is vital to avoid conflicting obligations, as is currently the case for companies and platforms.

section_path: Digital Fairness Act: Freelance Movement response to the open consultation

evidence_quotes.quote: reduce the ability of platforms to personalise or innovate in UX / operational efficiency

paragraphs.text: Furthermore, too strict prescriptive rules may reduce the ability of platforms to personalise or innovate in UX / operational efficiency while over-regulation might raise operational costs, which will in turn raise costs for consumers.

section_path: Digital Fairness Act: Freelance Movement response to the open consultation

evidence_quotes.quote: test which UX or personalisation features are safe or acceptable.

paragraphs.text: In order to adopt a regime that is workable, it is important to support working groups with industry and users, regulatory sandboxes and/or pilot programmes to test which UX or personalisation features are safe or acceptable. It would also be good to introduce harmonised guidelines (or official EU-level codes of conduct) that platforms can adhere to, so there is clarity and comparability. Exemptions or allowances for essential operational design features that are necessary for safety, navigation and privacy may also be required.

section_path: Digital Fairness Act: Freelance Movement response to the open consultation

paragraphs.text: In addition to our general concerns, we have specific points regarding personalisation practices and dynamic pricing.

section_path: Digital Fairness Act: Freelance Movement response to the open consultation

paragraphs.text: While any new rules must protect consumers from harmful practices, it is crucial to recognise that not all personalisation is harmful. In fact, it is often a pro-competitive tool that benefits SMEs and independent professionals alike. The European Commission should take into account the many benefits of personalisation - e.g. delivering timely, relevant, and tailored content/offers - and identify clear gaps before further restrictions on personalisation are introduced in the DFA.

section_path: Digital Fairness Act: Freelance Movement response to the open consultation

evidence_quotes.quote: DSA adding further protections against certain types of targeted advertising.

paragraphs.text: Existing safeguards - A multi-layered safety net is already in place, with the GDPR setting a strong baseline for data processing and the DSA adding further protections against certain types of targeted advertising.

section_path: Digital Fairness Act: Freelance Movement response to the open consultation

evidence_quotes.quote: Personalisation is key to the user experience on digital platforms that connect SMEs with consumers.
The platform might also suggest a specific category of services/goods based on the customer's past searches.

paragraphs.text: Pro-innovation and pro-SMEs - Personalisation is key to the user experience on digital platforms that connect SMEs with consumers. For example, when a consumer visits a platform, they might see a list of SMEs and professionals they have worked with before, making it easy to re-engage them. The platform might also suggest a specific category of services/goods based on the customer's past searches. This is not just a convenience for the consumer; it is a vital tool for the thousands of SMEs which rely on these data-driven recommendations to be discovered by new consumers.

section_path: Digital Fairness Act: Freelance Movement response to the open consultation

evidence_quotes.quote: use data from past interactions to tailor the user experience

paragraphs.text: Risk of overreach - Overly broad rules could stifle these benefits. If platforms were unable to use data from past interactions to tailor the user experience, the service would be less relevant for consumers, and new or specialised SMEs would struggle to gain visibility in a crowded marketplace.

section_path: Digital Fairness Act: Freelance Movement response to the open consultation

evidence_quotes.quote: Dynamic pricing is a standard and transparent practice that allows prices for a service to adjust in real-time based on supply and demand.

paragraphs.text: Dynamic pricing is a standard and transparent practice that allows prices for a service to adjust in real-time based on supply and demand. When properly implemented, it creates a more efficient and fair marketplace for both consumers and independent professionals. This practice is already sufficiently regulated in EU consumer law and should not be further tackled by the DFA. The focus should be on the enforcement of these existing rules.

section_path: Digital Fairness Act: Freelance Movement response to the open consultation

evidence_quotes.quote: Dynamic pricing allows independent professionals to increase their earnings during periods of high demand.

paragraphs.text: Empowering independent workers - Dynamic pricing allows independent professionals to increase their earnings during periods of high demand. This flexibility ensures their time and skills are valued appropriately, and it creates an incentive to make themselves available when their services are most needed by the community.

section_path: Digital Fairness Act: Freelance Movement response to the open consultation

evidence_quotes.quote: During periods of lower demand, prices can decrease

paragraphs.text: Benefiting consumers - This model also benefits consumers. During periods of lower demand, prices can decrease, making services more affordable and accessible. It helps balance the market, reducing waiting times for clients and maximising earning opportunities for independents.

section_path: Digital Fairness Act: Freelance Movement response to the open consultation

evidence_quotes.quote: For dynamic pricing to work fairly, transparency is essential.
Consumers should always be shown the final price upfront, before they commit to a purchase or service.

paragraphs.text: The key is transparency - For dynamic pricing to work fairly, transparency is essential. Consumers should always be shown the final price upfront, before they commit to a purchase or service. This empowers them to make an informed choice.

section_path: Digital Fairness Act: Freelance Movement response to the open consultation

evidence_quotes.quote: Rigid, one-size-fits-all rules on pricing could disrupt this delicate balance.

paragraphs.text: Risk of overreach - Rigid, one-size-fits-all rules on pricing could disrupt this delicate balance. Such regulations risk harming independent professionals by limiting their earning potential and harming consumers by reducing the availability of services when they are most needed.

section_path: Digital Fairness Act: Freelance Movement response to the open consultation

paragraphs.text: Freelance Movement is the largest stakeholder initiative in Europe focused on the future of work, with 49 members from across the continent. We understand the role that digitalisation is playing in the future of work and how it is creating opportunities, facilitating growth and driving the economy.

section_path: Digital Fairness Act: Freelance Movement response to the open consultation

paragraphs.text: Educate on, explain and quantify freelancing and the future of work.

section_path: Digital Fairness Act: Freelance Movement response to the open consultation

paragraphs.text: Identify challenges and barriers, then with decision-makers and other stakeholders work towards creating policy and regulatory solutions.

section_path: Digital Fairness Act: Freelance Movement response to the open consultation

paragraphs.text: Provide exposure and business opportunities within our future of work ecosystem - as well as a platform - for members and partners.

section_path: Digital Fairness Act: Freelance Movement response to the open consultation

paragraphs.text: The contribution of the Central European Lawyers Initiative (CELI) is provided in the attached document.
